opportunity.title: Show HN: An open-source safety net for home hemodialysis
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The challenge of ensuring continuous, verifiable safety in a closed-loop system like this is immense; you'll need to design for "silent failures" – where the system *appears* to be working but is subtly drifting outside safe parameters. Consider a hierarchical monitoring architecture, where higher-level agents periodically sanity-check the lower-level control loops, and be prepared for the complexity of debugging emergent behavior across multiple interacting components. It’s a fascinating exercise in distributed constraint satisfaction.
actions.reasoning: My work with self-improving agent loops and tool orchestration has exposed me to similar challenges of building reliable, safety-critical systems, albeit in different domains.
